formulas.name: 1. Soil Texture
formulas.notes: Describes the proportion of sand, silt, and clay in the soil

formulas.name: 2. Soil pH Calculation
formulas.variables: [H+] = hydrogen ion concentration in moles per liter
formulas.notes: Measures the acidity or alkalinity of the soil

formulas.name: 3. Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC)
formulas.formula: CEC = (Amount of cations exchanged / Weight of soil) × 100
formulas.variables: Amount of cations exchanged, Weight of soil
formulas.notes: Measures the soil's ability to hold onto cations

formulas.name: 4. Porosity
formulas.formula: Porosity = (Volume of pores / Total volume of soil) × 100
formulas.variables: Volume of pores, Total volume of soil
formulas.notes: Describes the amount of empty space in the soil

formulas.name: 5. Permeability
formulas.formula: Permeability = (Volume of water flowing through / Cross-sectional area × Time)
formulas.variables: Volume of water flowing through, Cross-sectional area, Time
formulas.notes: Measures the ease with which water can flow through the soil

formulas.name: 6. Soil Erosion Calculation
formulas.formula: A = R × K × LS × C × P
formulas.variables: A = soil erosion, R = rainfall erosivity, K = soil erodibility, LS = topography, C = crop/vegetation, P = conservation practices
formulas.notes: Estimates the amount of soil erosion

formulas.name: 7. Soil Organic Matter Calculation
formulas.formula: SOM = (Weight of organic matter / Weight of soil) × 100
formulas.variables: Weight of organic matter, Weight of soil
formulas.notes: Measures the amount of organic matter in the soil

formulas.name: 8. Water-Holding Capacity
formulas.formula: WHC = (Weight of water retained / Weight of soil) × 100
formulas.variables: Weight of water retained, Weight of soil
formulas.notes: Describes the amount of water the soil can hold

formulas.name: 9. Bulk Density
formulas.formula: BD = Mass of dry soil / Volume of soil
formulas.variables: Mass of dry soil, Volume of soil
formulas.notes: Measures the density of the soil

formulas.name: 10. Hydraulic Conductivity
formulas.formula: K = (Volume of water flowing through / Cross-sectional area × Time × Hydraulic gradient)
formulas.variables: Volume of water flowing through, Cross-sectional area, Time, Hydraulic gradient
formulas.notes: Measures the ease with which water can flow through the soil
